notes: Three stacked squid silhouettes capture the rough horizontal arrangement of the photo but lose the actual count (4-5 squids), omit nearly all arms/tentacles, and fill the bodies with fragmented photo-trace noise rather than disciplined FAO stipple.
specific_issues: wrong squid count (3 rendered vs 4-5 in photo)
arms and tentacles essentially missing — only stub fragments near the heads
interior covered in noisy disconnected line fragments (photo-trace artifacts) instead of clean stipple fields
stipple is sparse and not concentrated in the chromatophore patches visible in the photo
eyes rendered as solid dots without the almond outline typical of FAO plates
fin/mantle silhouettes simplified to smooth blobs, losing the tapered fin geometry of loligo
no rendering of suckers or tentacular clubs
overall reads as a degraded edge-detection trace rather than a scientific plate
